title: Rich Canadian Blueberry Pie
description: A flaky, light Canadian dessert featuring vibrant blue blueberries and a buttery crust, perfect for any occasion.
instructions: Roll out the shortcrust pastry on a floured surface to fit a 9‑inch pie dish. Place the crust in the dish and trim the edges, then chill for 10 minutes to firm up.
In a large bowl, combine blueberries, sugar, cornstarch, lemon juice, vanilla, blue food colouring, and a pinch of salt. Toss until the berries are evenly coated.
Transfer the blueberry mixture into the chilled crust, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Cover the pie with a second sheet of pastry, cutting slits in the top flap to vent steam, or use a lattice pattern if desired.
Bake for 45–50 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling bubbles through the slits.
Remove the pie from the oven and let it rest on a wire rack for at least 20 minutes before slicing.
Serve warm or at room temperature, optionally with a dollop of whipped cream.
